summary: Executed Phases 1-2 of the plan autonomously: added maildir-store = ~/Mail and multi-file-strategy = act-dir to both [gmail] and [logos] sections of the accounts.conf home.file block in modules/home/email/aerc.nix, directly after each source = line, plus the forward-compat comment (maildir-store deprecated on upstream aerc master via enable-maildir, but required on the installed 0.21.0) and the one-line comment recording that single-message a is deliberately left unprompted. home-manager build --flake .#benjamin passed (exit 0); the rendered accounts.conf was inspected directly and confirmed correct in both account sections, archive = unchanged, no maildir-account-path added. Phases 3-6 (live single/multi-file archive verification against real Gmail and Logos mailboxes, the finding-7 act-dir/INBOX-tab risk, and the follow-up decisions that depend on those live results) require driving aerc's interactive TUI and running mail-sync/mbsync against real mail -- both explicitly prohibited for this autonomous agent -- so they are marked [PARTIAL] in the plan with per-task deviation annotations and translated into a manual verification checklist in the implementation summary for the user to run themselves.
definition_of_done_note: Config applied + home-manager build passing is the autonomous definition-of-done here, per explicit task instructions. Full task completion (status COMPLETED) additionally requires the user to run the manual verification checklist against live mail.
next_command: User: home-manager switch --flake .#benjamin, then follow the manual verification checklist in summaries/01_enable-archive-action-summary.md
open_questions: Does archiving a known multi-file message succeed from the INBOX tab, or does it fail with 'refusing to act on multiple files' and require a :cf Gmail<Enter> switch? Deferred to the user's manual checklist run (Phase 4); determines whether a follow-up keybind change belongs in this task or a new one.

Does the act-dir multi-file archive leave an unpushed UID-stripped local duplicate (gmail-all is Create-Near-only), or does mbsync push a server-side duplicate? To be confirmed via the Gmail web UI after mail-sync gmail, per the manual checklist.
commit_note: No git add/commit was run by this agent. The working tree holds intermingled uncommitted hunks for tasks 111/113 in modules/home/email/aerc.nix; the final commit is handled by the orchestrator's consolidated commit.
